codeReview.summary: The change fully implements the feature contract by making the CSV fixture email match the uniquely registered test user, which removes the importer-identity mismatch under shared-schema execution without reintroducing a hardcoded colliding email.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-test-worker skill instructs workers to read `.factory/services.yaml`, but this worker first tried the non-repo path `/Users/opentoggl-ci/.factory/services.yaml` and hit ENOENT before proceeding. The skill could be clearer that the services file is repo-local so workers do not waste a step on the wrong absolute path.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton shows `Read /Users/opentoggl-ci/.factory/services.yaml` -> ENOENT, while the actual repo file is `/Users/opentoggl-ci/Code/opentoggl/.factory/services.yaml`.

summary: Reviewed handoff, transcript skeleton, and commit `2cf7bd8`. The implementation is a one-line fixture fix in `apps/backend/internal/bootstrap/import_routes_test.go` that replaces the hardcoded CSV importer email with `uniqueEmail`, matching the registered user identity. No blocking or non-blocking code issues found; feature should pass scrutiny.
